author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

All three recipe shortfalls closed.

**(1) B2 silent-no-op assertion.** Extended `sub2-wronganswer.sh::b2` to call `jjf show --json <id>` after every (status, verb) pair and assert the post-status against an expectation table derived from `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:1778-2266` (the `set_status`, `block`, `unblock`, `set_status` paths) and `docs/storage-format.md` §3. Added `expected_post_status()` and `is_expected_rejection()` helpers; cells where the verb is expected to reject (block/unblock on closed/abandoned) are checked separately for silent acceptance (exit 0 but state unchanged) AND for "wrong-direction" mutation (exit 0 but state flipped). 40-cell run is clean — no silent no-ops in jjf today. Evidence: `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/.scratch/b2/evidence/matrix-*-show-stdout`. Diff lives in `sub2-wronganswer.sh:33-225`.

**(2) C3 10MB body cap.** Filed `679444a` (sev:contract-drift, qa-redteam-2026-06-25): "no documented body-length cap; multi-MB bodies accepted silently." The body of `679444a` lays out the two acceptable resolutions (declare a cap with a typed `body_too_large` envelope, or declare unbounded as the contract in storage-format.md §3) and cites the C3 evidence at `.scratch/c3/evidence/c3-bigbody-*`.

**(3) D3 ConcurrentWrite hint snapshot.** Added a `d3b` variant to `sub4-contractdrift.sh:130-261` using the fallback recipe — `git clone "$bare" "$sib"; cd "$sib"; jj git init --colocate; jjf pull origin`. The plan documented `jj git init --git-repo=. --colocate` but those flags are mutually exclusive in jj 0.40; `--colocate` alone is the right form when the working dir already has a `.git/`. The race surfaced as `push_rejected` (correct typed kind, exit 1), and the substring probe matched `"retry"`, `"rejected"`, `"fetch first"`, and `` "run `jjf pull" `` in the `message` field. Full hint archived at `.scratch/d3b/evidence/d3b-hint-snapshot.txt`. The existing ticket `88e4d6b` already captures this exact finding fully (raw git stderr leaking through `message`, `details.hint` inconsistency vs `concurrent_write`); a cross-link comment landed on `88e4d6b` documenting the d3b reproduction. No new ticket for D3 — `88e4d6b` is the durable home.

## Recommendation

Close c67f162. The harness now meets the spec for B2 (post-status assertion); C3 has a tracked contract-drift ticket (`679444a`); D3 has a working repro recipe in d3b and a cross-link comment on the pre-existing `88e4d6b`. The round's coverage is now what the design called for.

Workspace tests pass (`cargo nextest run --workspace`: 505/505).

## Confidence

high. Both b2 and d3b were run from a clean scratch repo with the release binary and produced the expected (clean / finding) outputs; the C3 ticket is a documentation finding, not a behavior claim. Tests are green.

## Open follow-ups

- `679444a` (sev:contract-drift) — C3 body-length cap: spec decision needed (declare cap vs declare unbounded).
